title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

notes: Worked perfectly out of the box, playing up to Kvach. I'm giving platinum here because it works exactly as it would running on windows, however if you use HDR you can't use AA and there's no option for anisotropic filtering. If you're using Mesa drivers I recommend you paste, pp_jimenezmlaa_color=16 R600_TEX_ANISO=16 %command% into your launch options in steam. You get post process AA and anisotropic filtering; works fine for me, hopefully it works for others too.

title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

notes:
Runs perfectly. Better than Windows 10. No tweaks needed for it to run a vanilla game smoothly. 

Works well with mods including Unofficial patches. Some tweaks needed for OBSE Plugins (needs patch, see below), retextures, etc. Many work without any tweaks needed 

If modding, you may run into some issues with case-sensitive mods, this guide may help with some of those issues: https://en.uesp.net/wiki/Oblivion:Oblivion,_Mods_%26_Linux

Also, you need to make this script for your OBSE files to work correctly: https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=1587351870

title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

notes:
I didn't need to do anything special. Runs fine from either a fresh steamplay install or from an old, heavily modded install copied from my old Win HDD.

I haven't done much modding under Proton, but OBMM seems to be working perfectly. You have to swap the OBMM binary for the OblivionLauncher binary, but once done Steam runs it in the correct prefix with no fuss. To make the mods visible to OBMM you should copy them into the prefix in advance (e.g.: copy them to ~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/22330/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/My Games). You have to put the binaries back to normal after installing mods, because OBMM doesn't seem to be able to launch Oblivion on its own.

The previously mentioned install that was made under Windows also had the Nehrim mod installed, so I also tested running under Proton with Nehrim enabled. It ran that fine as well.
title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

notes: Runs out of the box on max settings. Also runs great using D9VK (PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command%). In older versions of proton the game runs, but with very low FPS in my computer. Using 4.11-6 version it runs great. HDR does not work, so I had to disable it on game menu options.
title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

notes: Works out of the box with good FPS at ultra settings. Only problem I had so far is that some of the numeric keys (used to shortcut weapons/spells) are not recognized. I still have 7 out of 10 which work, it's more than enough to play. Not worth downgrading to a silver rating.
title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition

gpu: ATI Technologies Inc. AMD Radeon VII
notes:
The game runs perfectly out of the box. I did however have the infinite walking bug. So there is one tweak that I did have to make, but I do not think that is enough to put it down to Gold. The one tweak I had to make to make the infinite walking bug go away was open up the file "Oblivion.ini" located at /home/[username]/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/22330/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/My Games/Oblivion


I then had to turn "bUse Joystick" from 1 to 0 (Essentially disabling the joystick).

After that, the game ran absolutely perfectly.
title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion® Game of the Year Edition
